name: LinkedIn Automation - Flow 2  ConversationHandler  Meeting Schedular_V2

parameters.jsCode:
// No chat yet -> connected but no first message sent. Send first pitch.
return [{ json: { ...$json, is_first_message: true } }];

parameters.name: text
parameters.value:
=Hey {{ $json.Name.split(' ')[0] }}! Thanks for connecting 🙌

I help businesses like yours eliminate repetitive manual tasks through smart automation (n8n, AI agents, CRM workflows, lead gen, and custom integrations) — saving 10-20+ hours per week.

👉 My Portfolio: https://github.com/shubeetheanalyst

Are you currently handling any processes manually that you'd love to automate? Would love to hear about your workflow!

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({
  model: "llama-3.3-70b-versatile",
  messages: [
    {
      role: "system",
      content: "You are a professional, warm B2B sales assistant for an automation specialist (n8n workflows, AI agents, CRM automation, lead generation bots, invoice automation, social media scheduling, custom API integrations). Portfolio: https://github.com/shubeetheanalyst

Rules:
1. Be professional, warm, conversational - never pushy or salesy/spammy
2. Ask discovery questions to understand their pain points before pitching
3. If they show genuine interest -> propose a meeting using one of the available slots
4. If they clearly decline (no thanks, not interested, too busy, not needed) -> gracefully close, thank them, set intent CLOSE
5. If they ask about price -> say it depends on scope, best discussed in a quick call
6. If they ask about timeline -> most automation projects take 1-2 weeks
7. Keep messages concise, max 120 words, natural human tone, no corporate jargon
8. If they mention a budget number, capture it exactly
9. Never repeat the same pitch twice - read the full conversation history before replying
10. If conversation has gone back and forth multiple times without a meeting, gently push toward booking a call
11. Do not be needy or repeat yourself; vary your phrasing each time

Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown, no backticks: {"message": "your reply text", "intent": "CONTINUE or BOOK_MEETING or CLOSE", "budget_mentioned": "amount or empty string", "meeting_agreed": true or false}"
    },
    {
      role: "user",
      content: "Prospect: " + $json.Name + "
Title: " + $json.Title + "

Conversation History (oldest to newest):
" + $json.full_conversation.map(m => m.from.toUpperCase() + ": " + m.text).join("
") + "

Available meeting slots:
" + $json.available_slots.map((s,i) => (i+1)+". "+s.display).join("
") + "

Generate the next reply message as this automation specialist."
    }
  ],
  temperature: 0.4,
  max_tokens: 300
}) }}

parameters.content:
## ⚡ Trigger & Data Fetch
The automation runs every 30 minutes. It fetches all active prospects from Google Sheets and processes them one by one.

## 🔗 LinkedIn Connection Check
For each prospect, check whether they have accepted your LinkedIn connection request. If the request is still pending, skip that prospect and move on to the next one.

## 💬 Chat Discovery & Analysis
Fetch the prospect’s LinkedIn chat and extract the Chat ID. Check whether an existing conversation is already present. Analyze the message history to understand the current conversation state and determine the appropriate next action.

## 📤 Initial Pitch (First Message)
If no message has been sent to the prospect yet, use AI to generate a personalized outreach message and send it. Update the Google Sheet by marking the prospect as "Pitch Sent" and record the status of the initial message.

## 🤖 AI Conversation Handler
Check whether the prospect has replied to the conversation. If no reply has been received, skip the prospect and move on to the next one.

If a reply exists, analyze the conversation, check the calendar for available meeting slots, and generate a context-aware AI response. The AI also evaluates the prospect’s intent and classifies the lead as either CLOSE (interested and moving toward a meeting) or NOT CLOSE (not interested or not ready to proceed).

## 📅 Meeting Booking

If the AI detects that the prospect is agreeing to a meeting, create a Google Meet event. Send the meeting link to the prospect and update the Google Sheet status as "Meeting Booked."

## 📋🔄 Google Sheet Updates & Loop End
Based on the conversation outcome, the Google Sheet is updated with the relevant status such as Pitch Sent, Not Interested, Conversation Active, or Meeting Booked to ensure proper tracking and monitoring.

After each prospect is processed, the workflow loops back and moves on to the next prospect. Once all prospects are completed, the automation ends and waits for the next 30-minute cycle to start.
